title: Family-level factors contributing to diet behaviors among Hispanics: opportunities for nutrition intervention
keywords: families preparing the new generation plus

summary.hook: This poster argues that family context is a key leverage point for improving diet behaviors and cardiometabolic health among Hispanic/Latinx households.
summary.summary_30s: This poster argues that family context is a key leverage point for improving diet behaviors and cardiometabolic health among Hispanic/Latinx households.

key_numbers: n=131 households; n=235 dyads; n=61 dyads.
Not stated on poster (parenting trial described as 10-week; multiple measurement time points T1/T2/T3 shown).
Families with elementary school-aged children (Athletes for Life; n=131 households).
Use of media during meals associated with adolescent weight (B=7.8, r=0.31, p=0.02) and BMIz (B=0.37, r=0.27, p=0.05).
Parental restrictive feeding style associated with adolescent weight (B=5.5, r=0.22, p=0.03), BMI% (B=13.2, r=0.52...
takeaways: Family matters because lifestyle behaviors are learned at home and shared; interventions can affect multiple family members; motivation includes being present for family...
